creator: thierry@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue  8 sep 09 at 15:47:25 +0200, flz@FreeBSD.org <flz@FreeBSD.org>
 wrote:
> Synopsis: Mk/bsd.port.mk: make ldconfig permanent for non-default prefixes.
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: flz
> State-Changed-When: Tue Sep 8 13:43:52 UTC 2009
> State-Changed-Why: 
> My understanding is that you're overriding PREFIX to be able to pass a
> different value to configure's --prefix option than the default
> ${PREFIX}. We could make this value default to ${PREFIX} if no value has
> been set already.  In any case, the fix you're offering is wrong and
> won't be committed.
> 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=112497

As far as I am concerned, you can close this PR.

I still think that ldconfig should consider the non-default PREFIXes,
but it does'nt matter anymore for net/mpich2: the latest incarnation of
this port does'nt redefine PREFIX.

Best regards,
-- 
Th. Thomas.
